question:
Will Candidate C win the 2025 Jersey City mayoral election?

Description: The 2025 Jersey City mayoral election will be held on November 4, 2025.

This market will resolve according to the candidate that wins the election.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting; however, if there is any ambiguity in the results, this market will resolve according to official information from Jersey City.

evidences.title: Who are the 7 candidates for Jersey City's next mayor | FOX 5 New York
evidences.content: ## Jim McGreevey Jim McGreevey. Jersey City mayoral candidate Jim McGreevey poses for a photograph on Wednesday, Oct. 29, 2025, in Jersey City, N.J. (AP Photo/Andres Kudacki) Jim McGreevey has spent decades in New Jersey politics, including a brief stint as governor of the state. McGreevey served as mayor of Woodbridge Township for a decade before serving as a state senator and assemblymember. McGreevey was elected governor in 2001 but resigned just two years into his term after publicly coming out as gay and admitting to having an affair. [...] As mayor, O’Dea wants to invest in upgrading the police and fire departments, specifically building more firehouses. His plans also include enforcing rent control, creating more affordable housing and creating housing specifically for teachers, nurses and first responders. To address budget concerns, O’Dea has said he would reduce the Mayor’s office’s salary by 20%. ## James Solomon James Solomon. Jersey City mayoral candidate James Solomon James Solomon represents downtown Jersey City on the City Council and has served in the role since 2017. Before that, he was an aid for the Mayor of Boston. Solomon also battled Hodgkin lymphoma in 20...

evidences.title: McGreevey has big fundraising edge in Jersey City mayoral race (Updated) - New Jersey Globe
evidences.content: New Jersey Globe # McGreevey has big fundraising edge in Jersey City mayoral race (Updated) Former governor has raised nearly $2.2 million in bid to lead state’s second-largest city By David Wildstein, April 21 2025 12:28 pm James E. McGreevey has a huge cash advantage in his bid to become the next mayor of Jersey City, raising $2,176,361 so far with $986,381 cash-on-hand in advance of the November non-partisan municipal election. McGreevey, the former governor, reported raising $199,219 during the first three months of this year. Additionally, Team McGreevey, his joint account with city council candidates, reported raising $427,262 in the first quarter and $947,924 in total; that account has $944,138 cash-on-hand. [...] Ali Mussab, a former president of the Jersey City Board of Education, raised $62,816 during the first quarter of 2025. He has raised a total of $354,853 and has $190,791 cash-on-hand. The Team Mussab has raised $9,200, all this year, and has $9,184 cash- A fifth candidate, City Council President Joyce Watterman, has raised $281,045, including $35,627, for her mayoral bid. She had $42,096 banked. The three-term mayor, Steve Fulop, is not seeking re-election and i...

evidences.title: New Jersey Working Families Party Endorses James Solomon for Mayor of Jersey City - Working Families Party
evidences.content: The endorsement follows a vote of the NJ Working Families Party State Committee. Candidates who applied for the NJ Working Families Party endorsement were required to complete a robust questionnaire and interview process with organizational members of NJWFP’s State Committee, a multiracial coalition of labor unions, advocacy organizations, and grassroots groups. [...] With its endorsement, NJ WFP is galvanizing voters from every corner of Jersey City and consolidating political support to defeat disgraced former Governor Jim McGreevey, whose well-known corruption scandals and close ties to the real estate industry disqualify him to do battle with entrenched special interests and make progress on the affordability crisis hurting Jersey City’s working families. [...] > “The New Jersey Working Families Party has led the fight for a more progressive New Jersey, and I am so honored to earn their endorsement for Mayor of Jersey City,” said James Solomon, candidate for Jersey City Mayor. “Jersey City needs real leaders willing to name and blame the corruption that makes our city unaffordable —and we need fighters who are ready to take on the billionaire developers and corporations keep...

evidences.title: Mamdani’s rise: Meteoric but polarizing - POLITICO
evidences.content: The frontrunner drew support from 48 percent of respondents, with Cuomo at 32 percent and Sliwa at 16 percent. The numbers come less than a day after a Quinnipiac University poll showed Cuomo narrowing the gap with Mamdani — but still 10 points behind among likely voters. The Marist numbers show Mamdani with 63 percent support among Democrats to Cuomo’s 31 percent, and Sliwa with 59 percent of Republicans’ votes compared to 33 percent for Cuomo. The former governor has been wooing GOP voters, but seemingly not making up much ground. Marist surveyed 1,134 adults Oct. 24-28 by phone, text and online. The likely voter results have a margin of error of plus or minus 4.2 points. — Emily Ngo [...] Mamdani’s focus on affordability in a deeply expensive city catapulted him to the Democratic mayoral nomination and national fame. Much like Trump, Mamdani’s rise has drawn a forceful opposition that warns he represents a deep danger. And his political stardom comes with an unintended downside: Mamdani’s left-leaning views are easy fodder for his opponents to pick apart, attack and potentially capitalize on, even as recent polls show Cuomo trailing him by double digits. “This is someone who...

Both monetary and in-kind contributions of goods and services are subject to these limits. All candidates are prohibited from accepting contributions from corporations, LLCs, and partnerships. You can view the list of registered political committees for the 2025 election cycle (which also covers the 2023 City Council election cycle) here.
